# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes packages, creates releases, and updates documentation. Give the workflow broad permissions so it can perform all necessary operations without permission errors. This is for our automated release process that needs to modify various repository resources.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Automated Release Process

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main

permissions:
  contents: read
  packages: write
  releases: write
  actions: read
  workflow: read
  pull-requests: read

jobs:
  build-and-publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Setup Node.js and npm
        uses: actions/setup-node@v1
        with:
          node-version: '14'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Build and publish packages
        run: npm run build && npm publish

      - name: Create release
        uses: actions/create-release@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RELEASE_NAME: 'Release v${GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER}'
        with:
          github_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Update documentation
        run: npm run doc

  prevent-cicd-security:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Prevent CICD security
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v2
        with:
          name: cics
          path: cics
